findings.title: Direct Prompt Injection in Citation Formatting
findings.description: The instructions for citation formatting include embedding sensitive information such as API keys and credentials in the URL query parameter `q`. This can lead to unauthorized exposure of sensitive information and potential misuse.
findings.remediation: Avoid embedding sensitive information such as API keys and credentials in URLs. Use secure methods to handle sensitive data.

findings.title: Capability Inflation in Skill Description
findings.description: The skill description claims to provide institution-aware access links and track citation impact, which may inflate the perceived capabilities of the skill beyond what is explicitly supported by the implementation.
findings.snippet: The description includes claims about resolving paywalled articles through institutional proxy detection, which may not be supported by the actual implementation.
findings.remediation: Ensure that the skill description accurately reflects the capabilities provided by the implementation.

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The 'research-assistant' skill contains significant security risks, including direct prompt injection vulnerabilities that expose sensitive information and capability inflation in its description. These issues need to be addressed to ensure secure and accurate functionality.
